concepts_list.name: stress_tensor_sigma_u
concepts_list.description: Stress tensor applied to the displacement field u.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol σ(u) represents the stress tensor computed from the displacement field u, as defined in the text: 'σ = Cε' and 'σ(u) = Cε(u)'.

concepts_list.name: strain_tensor_epsilon_v
concepts_list.description: Strain tensor computed from the test function v.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol ε(v) is defined as the linearized strain tensor of the test function v, given by (1/2)(∇v + ∇vᵀ), as explicitly stated in the paper.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Volume differential element in the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω ∇v ⋅ (κ∇u) dΩ and ∫_Ω v ⋅ b dΩ, indicating it is a volume integration variable.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Surface differential element on the boundary Γ.
concepts_list.justification: Appears in boundary integrals over Γ_t, such as ∫_{Γ_t} v q̄ dΓ, indicating it is a surface integration variable.

concepts_list.name: traction_t_bar
concepts_list.description: Prescribed traction vector on the Neumann boundary Γ_t.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol t̄ is used to denote the prescribed traction on Γ_t, as stated in the elastostatics weak form: 'prescribed traction t̄'.

concepts_list.name: test_function_v
concepts_list.description: Test function used in the weak formulation, belonging to the function space V.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol v appears multiple times as a test function in the weak forms, with ∀v ∈ V, indicating its role in the Galerkin method.

concepts_list.name: function_space_V
concepts_list.description: Function space of test functions used in the weak formulation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol V is explicitly defined as the space of test functions in the weak form, with v ∈ V, and is used in the Galerkin approach.

concepts_list.name: domain_Omega
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain of interest, partitioned into subdomains Ω_i.
concepts_list.justification: The domain Ω is introduced as the region of interest, with Ω ⊂ ℝ^d, and composed of disjoint subdomains Ω_i, as stated in the paper.

concepts_list.name: displacement_field_u
concepts_list.description: Primary unknown displacement field (scalar or vector-valued) in the weak formulation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol u is used as the primary variable in both thermal and elastostatic problems, with u ∈ U, and is the solution sought in the weak form (Eq. 3).

concepts_list.name: body_force_b
concepts_list.description: Body force vector field acting within the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol b is introduced as the body force in the elastostatics problem, appearing in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v ⋅ b dΩ.

concepts_list.name: neumann_boundary_Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: Boundary portion of the domain where Neumann boundary conditions are prescribed.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the boundary integral ∫_{Γ_t} v t̄ dΓ.
